title: Avenastrum compactum
taxonomy: Avenastrum compactum belongs to the family Avenaceae, commonly known as the oat grass or oat grass family. This family is a small one, consisting of about 200 species of grasses. The Avenastrum compactum is further classified under the genus Avenastrum, which is a monotypic genus meaning it has only one species. Avenastrum compactum is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is a small perennial grass species that is often found in rocky outcrops and mountain meadows.
uses: Avenastrum compactum is a plant species, but limited information is available regarding its medicinal uses.
